steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject of the verb is "Mathematician Grigori Perelman" — singular. The phrase "sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S. Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau" is a parenthetical insertion (set off by commas) and does not change the subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
A singular subject takes a singular verb: is. "Have been" (A), "are" (B), and "are being" (D) are all plural and don't agree with "Perelman."

That matches choice C — C is correct.
